authselect-test-framework
  • How-To Guides
  • Using SSH
  • View page source

Using SSH

The client role provides client.ssh() for interactive SSH sessions, used for example in GSSAPI sudo tests:

with client.ssh("user-1", "Secret123") as ssh:
    assert ssh.run(f"kinit user-1@{server.realm}", input="Secret123")
    assert "(root) /bin/ls" in ssh.run("sudo -l").stdout
    assert ssh.run("sudo /bin/ls /root")

The base role also implements client.ssh() for opening connections from any Linux role.

See also

ssh()
